macro_segments.title: Sukkah within a Sukkah and the criteria for invalidation

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the case of a canopy in a Sukkah and Shmuel's lenient ruling
The baraita contradicting Shmuel: either roof or ten tefachim invalidates
Resolution: distinguishing fixed structures from temporary tents
Application to Sukkah tachat Sukkah: why both criteria are needed

macro_segments.title: The kila tent and sleeping while naked: whether a temporary tent creates a separate space

micro_segments.title: Shmuel's ruling on sleeping in a kila and reciting Shema
Challenge from baraita: explicit prohibition on sticking head out
Resolution: the case is when the kila is ten tefachim high
Comparison to a house: permanent structures invalidate even below ten tefachim

macro_segments.title: Alternative formulation of Shmuel's rule: two criteria required for invalidation

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda's statement of Shmuel's position via two sides of the same coin
Challenge and response: the baraita on posts and poles
The distinction: posts are fixed to the bed, poles are more portable

macro_segments.title: Rava bar Rav Huna's lenient ruling and its basis in Rabbi Yehuda's principle

micro_segments.title: Rava bar Rav Huna permits sleeping in a kila even with roof and ten tefachim
Gemara identifies this with Rabbi Yehuda: temporary tent does not negate permanent Sukkah
Sleeping under a bed as precedent for Rabbi Yehuda's position
Distinction between bed and kila: bed is not made to serve as roof
The fundamental principle: Sukkah is permanent, temporary objects do not negate it

macro_segments.title: Mishnah on bending vines and gourds over the Sukkah and the principle of ta'aseh velo min ha-asuy

micro_segments.title: Mishnah: bending vine or gourd over Sukkah is invalid; adding kosher schach makes it valid
Or cutting the vine makes it valid: the question of ta'aseh velo min ha-asuy
The principle ta'aseh velo min ha-asuy: active making required, not indirect creation
The debate: Rav Yosef says cutting is enough; Rav says you must move the schach

macro_segments.title: Rav's position: cutting the vine is the act of making the schach

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna corrects Rav Yosef: it is Shmuel, not Rav, who requires moving
Rav and Shmuel both permit without moving; Rav Amram Chasida's story with tzitzit
Cutting strings is the making of tzitzit; similarly, cutting vine is making of schach

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's position and the debate on when cutting makes an object valid

micro_segments.title: Question: does Shmuel hold cutting is not making?
Shmuel teaches Rabbi Chiya's case: cutting at the end does not count as making
Alternative reading: Shmuel permits cutting after knotting, but only if knotting already valid
Challenge to Rav from baraita: suspending without cutting is invalid even after cutting
Rav's weak response: case involves partial attachment still visible

macro_segments.title: The debate on berries in hadas and its connection to ta'aseh velo min ha-asuy

micro_segments.title: Baraita: plucking berries from hadas in a bundle is invalid according to Rabbi Shimon ben Hotzadak
Gemara proposal: debate reflects whether cutting/plucking counts as making
Revised proposal: debate is whether ta'aseh applies to lulav at all
Third proposal: debate is whether lulav requires a binding bundle at all

macro_segments.title: The debate on whether lulav must be bound: Rabbi Yehuda vs. the Sages

micro_segments.title: Baraita: unbound lulav is valid according to the Sages; Rabbi Yehuda requires binding
Rabbi Yehuda's reasoning: learns requirement from the verse about bundle of hyssop
The Sages' view: binding is a mitzvah to beautify, not a requirement for validity
Question of double knot: whether aesthetic or halachic concept of bundling

topical_tags.term: Rabbi Yehuda's principle: temporary tent does not negate permanent Sukkah
Ta'aseh velo min ha-asuy (active making, not indirect creation)
Vine and gourd bending over Sukkah
